he saide vnto her: what wylt thou have? She saide vnto hym: graunte that these my two sonnes maye sytt/ won on thy right honde/ and the other on thy lifte honde in thy kyngdom.

Iesus answered/ ⁊ saide: ye wot not what ye axe.Are ye able to drinke of the cuppe that Ishall drīke of? And to be baptysed with the baptim/ that I shalbe baptysed with? They answered to hym: that we are.

He saide vnto them: ye shall drinke of my cuppe/ and shalbe baptysed with the baptim that Ishalbehaptysed with all. But to syt on my ryght honde/ or my lyfte honde/ is not myne to yeve you: but to them for whom yt is prepayred of my father.
⸿ And whēthe x.herde this/ they desdayned attthe ij. brethren.
But Iesus called them vnto hym/ and saide: Ye knowe/ that the lordꝭ of the gentyls have dominacion over thē/ And they that are greate/ exercise power over them.
Yt shall not t be so amonge you: But whosoever wylbe greate amōge you/ let him be youre mīster,
and whosoever wylbe chefe/ lett hym be youre servaunt.
Even as the sonne of man cam/ not to be mīstred vnto/ but to minister: and to geve hys lyfe for the redempcion of many.
⸿ And as they departed from hierico/ moche people folowed him.
And beholde/ ij.blynde mēsyttīge by the way syde/ whē they herde/ that Iesus passed by/ cryed sayinge: Master the sōne of dauid have mercy on vs.
And the people rebuked thē/ be cause they shulde holde there peace: But they cryed the moare/ sayinge: have mercy on vs master which arte the sonne of dauid.
Thē Iesus stode styll/ and called them/ and saide: what will yethat I shall do to you?
they saide vnto hym: master/ that oure eyes maye be opened.
Iesus petyed thē/ and touched there eyes.and immediatly there eyes receaved syght: And they folowed hym.
WHen they drewe nye vnto hierusalem/ and were come to betphage/ vnto mounte olivete/ thē sent Iesus ij. of his disciples,
sayinge to them: Go in to the toune that lyeth over agaynste you/ and anon ye shall fynde an asse bounde/ and her coltt with her/ lose them and brynge them vnto me.
And if eny man saye ought vnto you/ saye ye that youre master hath neade of them/ and streyght waye he wyll let thē go.
All thys was donne/ to fulfyll that which was spoken by the prophet/ sayinge:
tell ye the doughter of sion: beholde thy kynge cōmeth vnto the meke/ syttinge vppō an asse and a colte/ the foole of an asse vsed to the yooke.
The disciples wēt/ and dyd as Iesus cōmaunded them,
and brought the asse and the colte/ and put on thē there clothes/ and set hym there on.
Many of the people spreed there garmēttꝭ in the waye. other eut doune braūches from the trees/ and strawed them in the waye.
Moreover the people that wēt before/ 7 they also that cā after cryed sayinge: hosian̄a to the sonne of dauid. Blessed be he that commeth in the name of the lorde/ hosianna in the hyest.
⸿ And when he was come in to hierusalem/ all the cite was moved/ sayinge : whovsthys?
And the people s aide: this ys Iesus the prophet of nazareth a cite of galile.
And Iesus wēt in to the temple of god/ and caste out all thē that bought and solde in the temple/ And overthrew the tables of the mony chaungers/ and the seatꝭ ofthē that solde doves.
And saide to thē: it is written/ myne housse shalbe called the housse of prayer/ but ye have made it a den̄ of theves.
And the blynde and the halt cā to hym in the temple/ and hehealed them.
⸿ When the chefe preestꝭ and scribes sawe the marveylles that he dyd/ and the chyldren cryinge in the temple and sayinge/ hosianna to the sonne ofdauid/ they desdayned,
and sayde vnto hym: herest thou what these saye? Iesus saide vnto them: have ye never reed/ of the mouth of babbꝭ and suckelingꝭ thou haste ordened prayse?
And he lefte them/ and went out ofthe cite vnto bethany/ and passed the tyme there.
⸿ In the mornynge as he returned in to the cite ageyne/ he huugred,
and spyed a fygge treein the waye/ and cā to it/ and foundenothinge there on/ but leves only/ and said to it/ never frute growe vn the hence forwardꝭ. And anō the fygge tree wyddreda waye.
And whēhys disciples sawe that/ they mar velled sayinge: Howe soone is the fygge tree wyddred awaye?

Iesus answered/ and saide vnto them: Verely I saye vnto you/ yf ye shallhave faith/ and shall not dout/ ye shall not only do that which y have doe to the fygge tree : but also yf ye shall saye vnto this mountayne/ take thy silfe a waye/ and cast thy selfe in to the see/ it shalbe done.
And what soever thīge ye shall axe in youre prayers yf ye beleve/ ye shall receave hit.
⸿ And when he was come intothe temple/ the chefe preestꝭ ⁊ the seniorꝭof the people cā vnto hym as he was teachynge/ and saide: by what auctorite doest thou these thīgꝭ? and who gave the this power?
Iesus answered/ and saide vnto thē: I also wyll axe of you a certayne qnestiō/ which yf ye asoyle me/ y in lyke wyse wyll tell you by what auctorite I do these thingꝭ.
Whence was the baptī of Ihon? from heven/ or of men? And they thought in thēselves/ sayinge: yf we shall saye/ from hevē/ he wyll saye vnto vs: why dyd ye not them beleve hym?
but and yf we shall saye of mē/ then feare we the people. For all men helde Ihon as a prophet.
And they answered Iesus/ and saide: we cannot tell. He lyke wyse saide vnto thē: nether tell I you by what auctorite I do these thingꝭ.
Watsaye ye to thys? a certayne mā had ij. sonnes/ and cā to the elder sayinge: go and worke to daye in my vyne yarde.
he answered and sayd/ I wyll not: but afterwarde repented and went.
Then cam he to the seconde/ and saide lyke wise/ and he answered 7 sayde: I wyll syr: yet went he not.

Whydder of these ij. fulfylled there fathers wyll? And they saide vnto hym/ the fyrst. Iesus saide vnt o them: verely y saye vnto you/ that the publicās and the harlotꝭshall come into the kyngdom of god before you.

For Ihon cam vnto you/ in the waye of righte wesnes/ and ye beleved hym not. But the publicās and the whoores beleved hym. But ye (though ye sawe it) yet were nott moved with repentaunce/ that ye myght afterwarde have beleved hym.

⸿ Herken another similitude. There was a certayne housholder/ whych set a vyneyarde/ and hedged it rounde obout/ and made a vyn presse in it/ and bylte a tower/ and lete it out to husbandmen/ and went in to a straunge countre.
And when the tyme of the frute drewe neare/ he sent his servauntꝭ to the husbandmē/ to receave the frutꝭ of it,
and the husbandmen caught his seruauntꝭ/ and bet won/ kylled another/ stoned another.
Agayne he sent other servauntꝭ moo then the fyrst/ and they served them lyke wyse.
But last off all/ he sent vnto them hys awnesonne/ sayinge: they wyll feare my sonne.
When the husbandmen sawe hys sonne/ they sayde amonge them selves: Thys ys the heyre/ come on lett vs kyll him/ and lett vs take hys inherytaunce/ to oure selves.
And they caught hym and thrust hym out off the vyneyarde/ and shlewe hym.
When the lorde off the vyneyarde commeth : what wyll he do with those husbandmen?
They sayde vnto hym: he wyll evyll destroye those evyllpersons/ and wyll let out hys vyneyarde vnto other husbandmen/ whych shall delyver hym hys frute att tymes cōvenient.

⸿ Iesus saide vnto them: dyd ye never reede in the scripturꝭ/ the same stone which the bylders refused/ is set ī the princypall parte of the corner.this was the lordes doinge/ and yt ys mervelous in oure eyes.
Therfore saye I vnto you/ the kyngdom of god shalbe taken from you/ and shalbe gevēto the gētyls which shall brynge forth the frutes of it.
And whōso ever shall fall on thys stone/ shalbe alto broken. And whosoever this stond shall fall oppon/ he shall grynde hym to powder.
And when the chefe preestꝭ and pharyses herde his similitudes they perceaved that he spake of them.
And they went about to laye hondꝭ on hym/ but they feared the people/ because they counted hym as a prophet. 22:1 And Iesus answered and spake vnto them agayne/ in similitudes/ sayinge.
THe kyngdom of heven is lyke vnto a certayne kynge/ which maryed his sonne,
vnd sentt forth hys servauntꝭ/ to call thē that were byd to the weddinge/ and they wolde not come.
Ageyne he sentt forth other servauntꝭ/ sayinge: tell them which are bydden: Lo Ihaveprepared my dynner/ myne oxen and my fatlingꝭ are kylled/ and allthingꝭ are redy/ come vnto the maryage.
They made light of it/ and wēt there wayes: won to his ferme place/ another about his merchandyse.
the remnaunt toke his servauntꝭ/ and intreated them vngoodly/ and slewe them.
When the kynge herdethat/ he was wroth/ and sent forth his warryers and distroyed those murthres/ and brent vppe there cite.
⸿ Then saide he to hys servauntꝭ: The weddinge was prepared: but they which were bydden there to/ were not worthy.
Go ye therefore out in to the hye ways/ and as many as ye fynde/ byd them to the mariage.
The servaunttꝭ went out in to the wayes/ and gaddred togedder as many as they coulde fynde/ booth good and bad/ and the weddinge was furnysshed with gestꝭ.
The kynge cam in/ to viset hys gestꝭ/ and spyed there a man which had not on a weddinge garment,
and sayde vnto hym: frēde/ howe camyst thou in hydder/ and
